summary: Provision Amazon WorkSpaces to hand a user a managed cloud desktop. Creating a WorkSpace is an engineer-gated form — it's billed per-user and joins an existing directory, so it isn't blind self-service.

operations.title: Create a new WorkSpace
operations.summary: Request a new cloud desktop for a directory user. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_workspaces_workspace: the directory it joins, the user it's assigned to, the bundle (hardware/software image) it launches from, and its running mode. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: workspace_label
params.label: WorkSpace label
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names this WorkSpace in the generated draft — a WorkSpace has no name field of its own; it's identified by directory + user.

params.name: directory
params.label: Directory
params.help: The WorkSpaces directory this desktop joins. Register the directory first if the list is empty.

params.name: user_name
params.label: Directory username
params.help: The directory user this WorkSpace is assigned to. Must already exist in the directory.

params.name: bundle_id
params.label: Bundle
params.help: The hardware/software image this desktop launches from. The engineer picks a bundle ID from the account's available catalog during review.

params.name: running_mode
params.label: Running mode
params.help: AUTO_STOP stops the desktop after inactivity and bills hourly (cheaper for part-time use); ALWAYS_ON keeps it running and bills monthly.

params.name: root_volume_encryption_enabled
params.label: Encrypt root volume
params.help: Encrypts the operating-system volume at rest.

params.name: user_volume_encryption_enabled
params.label: Encrypt user volume
params.help: Encrypts the user's personal data volume at rest.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: WorkSpace name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Who this WorkSpace is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this WorkSpace.

decisions: The volume_encryption_key (a non-default KMS key for the encrypted volumes) is an engineer decision
IP access control groups and custom WorkSpaces Web settings are engineer follow-ups after creation
Registering the directory itself (aws_workspaces_directory), including its VPC/subnet placement, happens before this form is used — this form only assigns a desktop inside an existing directory
